subject: Databases
title: Indexes and Query Speed
description: A quick win: understand Indexes and Query Speed.

hook.text: A slow query ran for 8 seconds. Add one index. Same query, 4 milliseconds. Indexes are that powerful.

buildup.text: CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email). Now lookups by email skip the full table scan.

discovery.text: Composite indexes cover multiple columns: (country, city). Order matters — country first, then city.

twist.text: Indexes speed reads but slow writes. Each insert must update every relevant index. Balance is key.

climax.text: Use EXPLAIN to check if your queries actually use the indexes you created. Don't guess.

punchline.text: Indexes: the biggest lever for query speed. Use EXPLAIN to verify.

quiz.question: Why does column order matter in a composite index?
options: The database can only use the index efficiently for queries that match columns from left to right
Column order is alphabetical by default
It only affects how the index is displayed, not performance
